sentence_reviews.text: Expert and production-rule systems apply explicit if–then rules over symbolic facts, separating a curated knowledge base from an inference engine.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA knowledge-based agents, inference, and expert-systems material describes knowledge bases, inference engines, and rule-based reasoning.
sentence_reviews.notes: The standard expert-system architecture is supported; the exact combined wording is synthesized from AIMA topics.

sentence_reviews.text: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Knowledge graphs, ontologies & schemas, Constraint propagation, logic & program execution.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: Expert systems, production rules & rule engines consumes, emits, or organizes information using Knowledge graphs, ontologies & schemas.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA knowledge representation and knowledge-base material supports symbolic schemas/facts for expert systems.
sentence_reviews.notes: Knowledge bases are directly relevant, but the modern knowledge-graph label is broader than the inspected evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: Expert systems, production rules & rule engines is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Constraint propagation, logic & program execution.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA covers rule-based inference, logical reasoning, and constraint satisfaction topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Logic/inference support is clear; constraint-program execution is broader than expert systems alone.
